a:r.a:t: Three major subsystems define what makes this harness enterprise-grade rather than a simple chatbot. GraphRAG adds knowledge graph integration -- instead of just searching by text similarity, we can follow entity relationships across documents. The graph module has 10 files, a dedicated graph_manager agent with 12 tools, and supports both Microsoft GraphRAG and Neo4j backends. It uses a phased build pipeline: Phase 1 extracts entities and relationships via LLM, Phase 2 detects communities and generates reports, Phase 3 builds embeddings and indexes. Four query methods are available: local (entity-level), global (corpus-wide themes), drift (temporal change tracking), and SQL via a StructuredSearchAdapter. MCP -- Model Context Protocol -- enables dynamic tool discovery from external servers. It uses Fernet symmetric encryption for secrets, HTTPS enforcement to prevent SSRF attacks, and a tool deferral system where all MCP tools require explicit approval before execution. The Worker System provides multi-agent coordination with 14 orchestration tools. Workers run on separate threads with their own QueryLoop instances, communicate through a team mailbox pattern, and support durable jobs that survive server restarts. The coordinator agent manages up to 9 different worker agent types.
